texto: Some things can't be explained. They just are. And after a while they disappear, usually forever, or become interesting in another way. Literature's consolations are always temporary, while life is quick to begin again. It is better not even to look so hard, to leave off explaining. Nothing makes me more queasy than to spend time with people who don't know that and who can't forget, and for whom such knowledge isn't a cornerstone of life.

texto: Dreaminess is, among other things, a state of suspended recognition, and a response to too much useless and complicated factuality. texto: Finally I do like best of all stories whose necessity is in the implied recognition that someplace out there there exists an urgency—a chaos—, an insanity, a misrule of some dire sort which can end life as we know it but for the fact that this very story is written, this order found, this style determined, the worst averted, and we are beneficiaries of that order by being readers.

texto: Loneliness, I've read, is like being in a long line, waiting to reach the front where it's promised something good will happen. texto: They may already know too much about their mother and father--nothing being more factual than divorce, where so much has to be explained and worked through intelligently (though they have tried to stay equable). What could be lonelier for a parent than to be criticized by his child on a first-name basis?

texto: The saved moment is the true art of love.
explicacao: Citação de Richard Ford sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “The saved moment is the true art of love.” articula tempo, love, moments em registro de tempo, com relevância para contextos como moments.

texto: What's friendship's realest measure?I'll tell you. The amount of precious time you'll squander on someone else's calamities and fuck-ups.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Richard Ford que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “What's friendship's realest measure?I'll tell you. The amount of precious time you'll squander on…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amizade, reflexao, friendship, com foco em termino e reflexao.

texto: We are past the end of things now, but I don't want to leave.
explicacao: Citação de Richard Ford sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “We are past the end of things now, but I don't want to leave.” articula reflexao, end, finality em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como termino e finality.
